boundary: Design-layer decisions only. Source duplicate/conflict records remain unchanged.

conflicts.title: Fundamental quantities scope
conflicts.preferenceReason: Prefer wording scoped to Chapter 1 mechanics while retaining charge in the broader physics context.
conflicts.citationRepairNote: Citation defect identified 2026-07-16 (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_CITATION_REPAIR.md): sourceRecordIds previously cited SEG004, independently verified to map to ch01-t14, not ch01-t01, in docs/content-audits/chapter-01/topic-mapping.json. Corrected evidence set now cites S2-SEG026 (primary), SEG009, SEG010, S3-P003, S3C-Q001, R1-Q001, and (in scientificAuditRecordIds) adds K1-SCI-004. The scope-clarification issue this conflict record describes remains substantively resolved by ch01-corr-001's corrected wording, which was not changed by this repair. Project-owner decision: Revise, scope: evidence citation and traceability repair only. ch01-corr-001 remains approvalStatus: proposed; this conflict record remains resolutionStatus: proposed (not resolved) pending a separate future approval decision.
conflicts.approvalUpdateNote: ch01-corr-001 received project-owner editorial approval on 2026-07-16 (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_APPROVAL_RECORD.md). The citation repair recorded in citationRepairNote above is confirmed intact and unchanged as of this approval. The proposed correction wording was approved as-is, with no substance change. Independent human subject-matter (physics) review has not occurred — this is project-owner editorial approval only; SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json's ch01-corr-001.approvals.scientificReviewer remains null. No ch01-t01 canonical content record exists yet, so this approval does not itself change canonicalPreferenceStatus, preferredSourceRecordId, or resolutionStatus above — consistent with CD-CONF-006/007/008's own precedent of remaining unchanged after their linked corrections were approved, since a design-layer preference is only applied once actual canonical content exists to apply it to. Student-facing publication is not authorized by this approval.

conflicts.title: Mass, gravitational weight, and apparent weight terminology
conflicts.preferenceReason: Use mass as inertia, distinguish gravitational weight from apparent weight, and retain amount-of-matter wording only as introductory intuition.
conflicts.citationRepairNote: Citation defect identified 2026-07-16 (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_DECISION_BRIEF.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_CITATION_REPAIR.md): sourceRecordIds previously cited SEG017 and SEG018, independently verified to map to ch01-t03, not ch01-t04, in docs/content-audits/chapter-01/raw-sources/source-001-segments.json. R1-Q004 was already correctly cited here (unlike in ch01-corr-002's own prior sourceRecordIds, which cited the miscited S5-P033 instead — now aligned to match this record). Corrected evidence set now cites SEG021, SEG022 (primary), S2-SEG056, S2-SEG058, R1-Q004, and (in scientificAuditRecordIds) adds K1-SCI-010 as a positive-contrast corroboration. The mass-as-inertia / gravitational-weight-versus-apparent-weight interpretation this conflict record describes remains substantively resolved by ch01-corr-002's corrected wording, which was not changed by this repair. Project-owner decision: Revise, scope: evidence citation and traceability repair only. ch01-corr-002 remains approvalStatus: proposed; this conflict record remains resolutionStatus: proposed (not resolved) pending a separate future approval decision.
conflicts.approvalUpdateNote: ch01-corr-002 received project-owner editorial approval WITH QUALIFICATION on 2026-07-16 (see docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_CORRECTION_APPROVAL_RECORD.md). The citation repair recorded in citationRepairNote above is confirmed intact and unchanged as of this approval. The proposed correction wording was approved as-is, with no substance change, subject to this qualification: 'apparent weight' is project-authored interpretive terminology, scientifically valid and consistent with SCA03's own recommendation, but not represented as verbatim wording from the cited source material (no cited source uses that exact phrase). Independent human subject-matter (physics) review has not occurred — this is project-owner editorial approval only; SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json's ch01-corr-002.approvals.scientificReviewer remains null. No ch01-t04 canonical content record exists yet, so this approval does not itself change canonicalPreferenceStatus, preferredSourceRecordId, or resolutionStatus above — consistent with CD-CONF-006/007/008's own precedent. Student-facing publication is not authorized by this approval.

conflicts.title: Perpendicular resultant interpretation
conflicts.preferenceReason: Replace the invalid right-angle-addition justification with a complete vector-sum example.

conflicts.title: Graph concavity wording ambiguity
conflicts.preferenceReason: State whether slope increases or decreases and whether the graph is concave up or down under an explicit axis convention.

conflicts.title: Usain Bolt event and time mismatch
conflicts.preferenceReason: Do not combine Berlin 2009 with 9.69 s. The proposed factual pairing is Berlin 2009 = 9.58 s and Beijing 2008 = 9.69 s.

conflicts.title: Period/frequency cycle-definition requirement
conflicts.preferenceReason: Require an explicitly named periodic process and an explicit definition of one complete cycle before stating or applying the reciprocal relationship between period and frequency.

conflicts.title: Acceleration sign convention and |g| versus signed acceleration
conflicts.preferenceReason: Require an explicit positive-axis declaration before assigning a sign to acceleration; separate the magnitude |g| (~9.8 m/s²) from the signed acceleration component, which is -g under an upward-positive convention or +g under a downward-positive convention, and keep displacement/velocity/acceleration signs mutually consistent for free-fall motion from rest.

conflicts.title: Centripetal acceleration: tangent velocity versus inward acceleration
conflicts.preferenceReason: State that velocity remains tangent to the circular path at every instant and that centripetal acceleration (the rate of change of that velocity) points toward the center; never describe velocity itself as directed inward, and make explicit that constant speed does not imply constant velocity.

applicationRule: No preference is applied to canonical content until the linked correction is scientifically and editorially approved.
